key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that are adherent and still retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after processing.
engineering_relevance: This enables wear- and function-active NiTi surface layers on steel components, offering a promising alternative to brittle ceramic coatings for adaptive thermal and mechanical actuation.
method: NiTi coatings were deposited on mild steel by high-velocity air fuel (HVAF) thermal spraying.
Four different spray parameter sets were compared.
Coatings were analyzed for composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the 100–300 µm coatings showed martensitic transformation, thermal actuation in 3-point bending, and shape-memory behavior in nanoindentation and scratch tests.
watch_item: The as-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and require annealing to fully develop functional thermomechanical properties.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
